si.t: 1) failure to select a pinmux option for a given ball
2) bringing out the same signal on more than one ball
3) bringing out signals from more than one interface for a single controller

This spreadsheet automatically highlights many kinds of pinmux configuration problem. Errors are highlighted in pinkish-red on the MPIO, Signals, or Controllers worksheets. This spreadsheet highlights the following problems:
The only cells that need to be edited are the orange highlighted cells on the MPIO worksheet.

ALL NVIDIA DESIGN SPECIFICATIONS, REFERENCE BOARDS, FILES, DRAWINGS, DIAGNOSTICS, LISTS, AND OTHER DOCUMENTS (TOGETHER AND SEPARATELY, “MATERIALS”) ARE BEING PROVIDED “AS IS.” NVIDIA MAKES NO WARRANTIES, EXPRESSED, IMPLIED, STATUTORY, OR OTHERWISE WITH RESPECT TO THE MATERIALS, AND EXPRESSLY DISCLAIMS ALL IMPLIED WARRANTIES OF NONINFRINGEMENT, MERCHANTABILITY, AND FITNESS FOR A PARTICULAR PURPOSE. However, NVIDIA Corporation assumes no responsibility for the consequences of use of such information or for any infringement of patents or other rights of third parties that may result from its use. This publication supersedes and replaces all information previously supplied. Customer Usage Description or Net Names
These can be switched depending on the function you want to use, which is defined by device tree.
The definitions in the device tree file are read by MB1 boot loader and loaded into each pin IO pad register on SoC.
Pinmux Table helps you to simplify the configuration work.
If this file is obtained by downloading it via Internet, the script performing device tree generation might be prohibited in some operating environments.
Please check the Security of 'Unblock' in the file's properties to allow script execution.
The target circuit should be designed according to the requirements defined by Design Guide and Datasheet.
Reflects the conditions of the module pins and connected circuits from the carrier board schematics to the respective pin settings.
There are setting combinations that is inappropriate e.g. External pull-up/pull-down and internal pull-up/pull-down.
Correct the part when appears the error as following;
Once the required design settings have been completed, press the ‘Generate DT file’ button above the table.
A pop-up window will ask you ‘Would you like to generate device tree file for pinmux table’.
Select 'Yes' and proceed, a dialogue box will appear where you can enter the name of the board to be defined in the generated file.
After entering the name, press OK and you will be asked for the board name again.
If you select 'Yes' again, a dialogue will appear informing you that three files have been created in the same directory of this file and the device tree generation is completed.

r.t: is a Pinmux table based on the defined values
is the default definition and the last file
is IO pad voltage definition device tree file for each pin.

si.t: The generated files are valid by placing it to the file system referenced by target board's boot loader.
Module pin location on B2B connector
The assigned name of signal on the pin
The pin referred to in the Wake up event. Cannot be changed.
DDR memory chip type which used on the module, Entering recovery mode, etc.
The status of these configuration pins is activated and loaded before the boot loader is activated by the dedicated module internal power supply.
Type of Pad IO Category for reference. Cannot be changed.
Internal pull-up or pull-down strength. Cannot be changed.
Shown pin state when POR default. Cannot be changed.
Select the functionality to be used. The default is the setting in Devkit.
Pins not in use should be set to ‘Unused’.
Specifies the direction of the signal at the MPIO port. Select from between input, output and Bidirectional.

r.t: is selected in the adjacent ‘Customer usage’, ‘
’ is automatically selected in grey italics.

si.t: Override POR setting; you can select from Z(floating), Int PU, Int PD, Drive0 and Drive1.
Drive the pin with Logic Low
Drive the pin with Logic High
Specifies whether the pin is used or not during wake-up operations.
This setting makes the pin state an open-drain drive.
It cannot be used as 3.3V tolerance enable on Thor SoC. Only a limited number of pins have 3.3V signal capability.
Enter a value here when the designed circuit uses an external pull-up resistor.
This is for the designer's reference only and is not reflected in the device tree.

r.t: If this entry is made when an internal pull-up is specified in the
, an error will occur and the relevant cell will turn pink.
. Please replace the word pull-up with pull-down.

si.t: Specifies the pin state during deep sleep operation.
It can be unspecified when not to use
The column is not reflected in the device tree.
It is useful to note what purpose is being used and the name of the signal to be connected.
Explanation of what the Devkit default settings are used for.
If you need help with this file, please ask the NVIDIA Developer Forums or local AE.
On Jetson Thor series module, there are multiple functions assigned to single pin.
For more detailed information, refer to Jetson Thor series Pinmux Table Application Note.
The next page as the default settings for Jetson Thor engkit and devkit carrier board.
Fix missing "Wake" selections in the "WAKE Pin" Colum. Change PAC.02 to Drive0. Add Jeton Thor Devkit (Jedha).
Consolidate tabs into Jeton_Thor_DevKit tab. Add DPAUX signals to the DTSI output file. Remove the Verilog Ball Name description from the Quick Guide.
This spreadsheet is a companion to the Jetson Thor product module and Developer Kit datasheet. It is designed to help system designers specify the pinmux configuration for each new system design.
Removed not supported pinmux options and aligned with Jetson Thor Design Guide and the Jetson Thor DevKit
Enabled MIPI CSI. Restored supported GPIOs. Note that all FSI_GPIOs are not supported
QSPI0 Drv Enabled. Add columns (BD,BE) to indicate internal PU/PD. Updated the Ext PU/PD values. Updated the Thor CVM Connector name (lines 152 and 153)
Change the usage of MODULE_SHDN_N to unused. Update the Req.Initial State to Z for HPD0-3. Update the FAN_TACH1 configuration.
In case of operating frequency exceeds 25 MHz, please modify the setting to 'Enable'.
Changing this setting to 'Enable' will improve signal integrity and ensure reliability operation.
Unlocked the DRV_TYPE selection for SPI. Added DRV_TYPE description in the Quick Guide.
Unhided column W to see the wake number.
